name: Kindergarten Anatomy & Physiology — Semester 1
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy through a close look at five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy through a guided practice with five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy.

units.title: Wonder and notice: five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy

activities.title: A close look at five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in five senses, body parts, healthy routines, and privacy.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
